title: 10 best herbal essential oils for chills
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for chills are natural remedies derived from plants that help alleviate chills and related symptoms such as fever, body aches, and fatigue. These oils are effective due to their unique chemical composition, which provides antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. Some of the best herbal essential oils for chills include Eucalyptus globulus, Zingiber officinale, Rosmarinus officinalis, Thymus vulgaris, Echinacea purpurea, Cinnamomum verum, Ginkgo biloba, Melaleuca alternifolia, Cymbopogon citratus, and Lavandula angustifolia. If left untreated, chills can lead to dehydration, exhaustion, and a weakened immune system, significantly impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with chills because of its unique composition, rich in eucalyptol and other beneficial compounds. Its invigorating aroma stimulates blood circulation, helping to warm the body from within. As the oil is absorbed into the skin, it also relaxes muscle tension, reducing shivering and promoting a sense of comfort and well-being. Additionally, its decongestant properties help clear airways, making breathing easier and more effortless.
remedy_properties: Decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties: Eucalyptol, one of the main components of Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil, helps to relieve congestion by reducing swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to feelings of chilliness.

Antimicrobial properties: The oil's antimicrobial activity can help combat infections that may be causing chills, such as respiratory viruses or bacterial infections, by inhibiting the growth of harmful microorganisms.

Expectorant properties: Eucalyptus globulus essential oil has natural expectorant properties, which means it helps to loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion that can contribute to chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: Tasmanian Blue Gum roots contain compounds that help to relax the body and reduce fever, making them effective in soothing chill-related symptoms.
Barks: The barks of Tasmanian Blue Gum trees have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to combat infections and reduce inflammation associated with chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves and rinse them gently under cold running water.
Dry the eucalyptus leaves in a warm oven at 50°c for 24 hours or until they reach moisture levels below 10%.
Crush 5 grams of dried eucalyptus leaves into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steam distill the powdered eucalyptus leaves with 100 ml of water for 2 hours at atmospheric pressure.
Filter the essential oil from the distilled mixture through cheesecloth and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with chills because of its calming and soothing properties. The oil's sweet, floral scent can help to ease tension and promote relaxation, which can in turn reduce feelings of chilliness and shivering. Additionally, the antispasmodic properties of English lavender essential oils can help to calm muscles and nerves, further alleviating symptoms of chills. By incorporating English lavender essential oils into your daily routine, you may find that you're better able to manage and overcome episodes of chills and other cold-related discomforts.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Helps to relieve muscle cramps, spasms, and stiffness associated with chills by relaxing tense muscles and improving blood flow.
Anti-inflammatory property: Reduces inflammation and swelling caused by chills, which can ease discomfort and promote faster recovery.
Calming and sedative property: Promotes relaxation and reduces stress levels, which can help alleviate symptoms of chills such as fever, shivering, and restlessness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which provide a calming and relaxing effect that can help alleviate chill symptoms.
Flowers: Lavender flowers are a popular choice for making essential oil due to their gentle, soothing properties that can ease chill-induced anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers at dawn for optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested lavender flowers using a dehydrator on low heat setting within hours.
Steam distill the dried lavender flowers in a stainless steel still with 50g of flowers per liter of water at atmospheric pressure.
Collect the resulting essential oil after 2-3 hours of steam distillation and separate it from the water via sedimentation.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities before storing it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with chills because of its natural warming properties, which can help to increase circulation and relax the body. The oil's antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds work together to ease congestion and reduce fever, making it an effective natural remedy for fighting off colds and flu. Additionally, thyme's decongestant properties help to clear mucus from the airways, allowing for easier breathing and a quicker recovery.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Thyme essential oil has antiviral properties that can help combat viral infections, such as the common cold or flu, which often cause chills.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce fever and alleviate body aches, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving chill symptoms like shivering and sweating.

Decongestant properties: The decongestant properties of thyme essential oil can help relieve congestion in the nasal passages and sinuses, which is often associated with chills, by reducing mucus production and opening airways.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are known for their warming and soothing properties, making them ideal for reducing chill and promoting overall body warmth.
Buds: Thyme buds contain a high concentration of essential oil compounds that help to stimulate circulation and relieve cold sensations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest thymus vulgaris leaves from healthy plants at peak flowering stage.
Dry the leaves in a well-ventilated area or using a drying rack for 7 days.
Crush dried thymus vulgaris leaves into small particles using mortar and pestle.
Steam distill crushed plant material in a 1:5 ratio with water for 90 minutes.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth or coffee filters.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with chills because of its natural ability to calm the body's nervous system. The oil contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have a soothing effect on the skin and muscles, helping to reduce feelings of coldness and tension. Additionally, lemon balm's citrusy scent can uplift and comfort the mind, promoting relaxation and ease. When inhaled or applied topically, herbal lemon balm essential oils can provide rapid relief from chills, leaving you feeling warm, cozy, and peaceful.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Herbal lemon balm essential oil has antiviral properties that can help to combat viral infections, such as the common cold and flu, which often cause chills.
Antispasmodic properties: The essential oil's ability to relax muscle spasms can help alleviate symptoms of chills, such as shivering and trembling, by reducing muscle contractions.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal lemon balm essential oil is rich in antioxidants that can help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by infections or other factors, which may contribute to chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help reduce fever and alleviate body aches associated with chills.
Leaves: Containing citral and other bioactive compounds, the leaves have natural analgesic properties that can help soothe cold-related discomforts.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and stems, ensuring they are free of pests and diseases.
Dry the harvested plant material using a warm air dryer at a temperature not exceeding 35 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material in a stainless steel still at atmospheric pressure using 1:5 ratio of water to plant material for 30 minutes.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water by decantation, allowing it to settle at the bottom of a container for 2 hours before transferring to an amber glass bottle.
Store the extracted melissa officinalis essential oil in a cool dark place at room temperature for 7 days before filtering through activated charcoal to remove impurities and contaminants.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curry plant essential oils helps with chills because of its natural warming properties. The oil contains compounds such as curcumin, which has been shown to stimulate circulation and increase body temperature. When applied topically or inhaled, the oil can help to warm the body from the inside out, providing relief from cold and shivering sensations. Additionally, the oil's anti-inflammatory properties can also help to reduce discomfort and alleviate symptoms of chills associated with illnesses like the common cold.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic property: Curry plant essential oil has been shown to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of chilliness by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are responsible for inducing inflammation and increasing body temperature.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: The antimicrobial properties of curry plant essential oil can help combat viral and bacterial infections that may be causing chills, reducing their severity and promoting recovery.

Inflammation-reducing property: Curry plant essential oil's anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce inflammation in the body, which is often associated with symptoms such as chills, fever, and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots of the curry plant are excellent for making essential oils that help alleviate chills and fever.
Barks: The bark of the curry plant contains phenolic compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, making it an effective component in essential oils for soothing chills.
Seeds: Curry seeds are known to possess antimicrobial properties, which can be beneficial in essential oil blends aimed at reducing the severity of chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh helichrysum italicum leaves in the early morning for maximum oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried helichrysum italicum leaves using 20% water and 80% carrier oil ratio for 30 minutes.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter to remove impurities and sediment for 15 minutes.
Bottle the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with chills because of its natural warming and decongestant properties. When inhaled, the oil's chemical compounds stimulate the body's thermoregulation process, helping to increase blood circulation and warm the body from within. Additionally, rosemary's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce congestion in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier breathing and a sense of relief from chills. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective way to combat the discomfort and symptoms associated with chills.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Rosemary essential oil helps to relax muscles and alleviate spasms, which can contribute to the relief of chills by calming the body's nervous system.

Expectorant: Rosemary oil has expectorant properties, which means it can help loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs and respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion that may be exacerbated by chills.
Antiviral: Rosemary essential oil has antiviral properties, which can help combat viral infections such as the common cold or flu, that may cause chills, fever, and other symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of rosemary contain bioactive compounds that can help alleviate chills by promoting sweating and reducing body temperature.
Buds: Rosemary buds are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help combat fever and ease chilliness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and stems from healthy plants.
Dry the harvested material in a single layer at room temperature for 7 days.
Steam distill the dried rosmarinus officinalis material in a 5:1 ratio with distilled water.
Collect and filter the essential oil using a paper coffee filter or cheesecloth.
Store the resulting essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with chills because of its natural warming properties, which help to increase blood circulation and relax muscles. The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds also work to reduce fever and ease congestion, allowing the body to naturally warm up and recover from a chill. Additionally, fennel's calming effects can help alleviate anxiety and stress, common triggers for chills, promoting overall relaxation and well-being. As a result, using herbal fennel essential oils can provide fast and effective relief from chills and feverish feelings.
remedy_properties: Decongestant: Fennel essential oil helps to relieve congestion and reduce nasal secretions, which can be particularly beneficial when dealing with chills caused by a cold or flu.
Antispasmodic: Fennel essential oil's ability to relax muscles and ease spasms can help alleviate symptoms such as body aches, cramps, and tension associated with chills.
Anti-inflammatory: Fennel essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the throat, sinuses, and other areas affected by chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots contain a high concentration of essential oil, which helps to warm and soothe the body, making them effective in relieving chills.
Seeds: Fennel seeds are often used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues, including chills, due to their expectorant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried fennel seeds from mature plants weighing 100-150 grams and store them in an airtight container.
Steam distill the dried fennel seeds using a steam distillation apparatus for 4-5 hours at a temperature of 100-105 degrees celsius.
Collect the resulting essential oil in a separate container and filter it through a paper filter or coffee filter to remove impurities.
Dilute the essential oil with a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil at a ratio of 10% fennel oil to 90% carrier oil.
Store the finished herbal essential oil blend in dark glass bottles and keep it refrigerated for up to 2 years while labeling it clearly with ingredients and preparation dates.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with chills because of its natural cooling properties, which can help to reduce body temperature quickly. When inhaled or applied topically, peppermint oil stimulates blood flow and circulation, which can help to warm the body from within. Additionally, peppermint's invigorating scent can also stimulate mental alertness and energy, helping to combat fatigue and feelings of chilliness. By promoting a sense of calm and relaxation, peppermint essential oils can provide natural relief from chills and shivering.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Peppermint essential oil helps to stimulate digestion, which can alleviate digestive issues that may be contributing to feelings of chilliness and discomfort.
Natural Cooling Agent: The menthol content in peppermint essential oil has a natural cooling effect on the skin, which can help to reduce fever and relieve symptoms of chills and heat exhaustion.

Decongestant and Anti-Inflammatory: Peppermint essential oil's decongestant properties can help to clear congestion and sinus pressure, while its anti-inflammatory properties may aid in reducing inflammation that can contribute to feelings of chilliness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, peppermint roots have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and relieve symptoms of chills.
Leaves: The leaves of the peppermint plant contain menthol, a key compound responsible for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in soothing chill-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 pounds of fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or local market during peak growing season.
Clean and dry the harvested peppermint leaves by gently blowing off excess moisture with compressed air.
Steam distill the dried peppermint leaves in a 4:1 water-to-herb ratio using a double-boiler setup for 2 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles within an hour of distillation.
Store the freshly extracted mentha x piperita essential oil in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months without exposure to direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram essential oils helps with chills because of its natural warming and soothing properties. The oil's sweet, herbaceous aroma is believed to stimulate circulation and reduce feelings of coldness or discomfort. It also possesses anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate tension and relax the muscles, making it an excellent remedy for relieving chilliness and promoting overall warmth and comfort. When inhaled or applied topically, sweet marjoram essential oil can provide a sense of calm and tranquility, helping to reduce stress and anxiety associated with chills.

remedy_parts: Roots: Sweet marjoram roots have been traditionally used to treat fever and cold-related symptoms, making them a great choice for creating medicinal essential oils that target chills.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried oregano leaves in sufficient quantity for desired oil production taking note of soil quality.
Dry the harvested oregano leaves using air circulation or machine drying methods within 24 hours of harvest ensuring optimal retention.
Steam distill the dried oregano leaves in a dedicated essential oil distillation apparatus at temperatures between 100-150 degrees fahrenheit processing 1 kilogram.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through paper filters or cotton cloths to remove impurities after distillation taking about an hour.
Store the origanum majorana essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids away from direct sunlight within a temperature range of 50-70 degrees fahrenheit.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bay essential oils helps with chills because of its warming and soothing properties. The oil contains a compound called linalool, which is known for its ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. When inhaled or applied topically, bay essential oil can help to calm the body and mind, reducing feelings of chilliness and discomfort. Its warm, spicy aroma also helps to stimulate circulation, which can help to increase body temperature and alleviate shivers. Stimulant: Bay essential oil is also a natural stimulant, which can help to warm the body from within by increasing blood flow and circulation, providing relief from cold and clammy sensations associated with chills.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh laurel leaves from mature trees early morning.
Dry the harvested leaves in shade or using desiccant for 7-10 days.
Steam distill dried laurel leaves using still apparatus and water at.
Collect distilled essential oil and separate it from hydrosol after 60 minutes.
Filter and bottle the extracted laurus nobilis essential oil within 24 hours.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat chills with herbal essential oils is to apply topically a blend of warming and calming oils such as ginger, cinnamon, and eucalyptus. Mix 2-3 drops of each oil with a carrier oil like coconut or sweet almond oil and massage onto the palms of your hands before inverting to warm up the body. This promotes blood circulation and helps to dispel chills naturally.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chills that are treated with herbal essential oils are fever, influenza, and body aches. Peppermint oil is often used to reduce fever and alleviate body aches, while eucalyptus oil helps to ease congestion and coughs associated with the flu. Ginger oil, known for its warming properties, can also help to relieve chills and joint pain. These oils are typically blended with other herbs and applied topically or inhaled through steam inhalation for maximum relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with chills because of its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. It works by thinning out mucus in the airways, making it easier to expel and relieve congestion. Additionally, its cooling sensation can help to reduce body temperature and provide relief from the discomfort of chills. The oil's expectorant properties also help to break down and clear out phlegm, allowing the body to recover from illness more efficiently. This leads to a sense of comfort and relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Acts as a natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory agent to help alleviate chills and reduce fever.
Pinene: Exhibits antiseptic and expectorant properties, helping to clear mucus and relieve respiratory congestion associated with chills.
Bornyl acetate: Shows antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms of chills.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Eucalyptus globulus for essential oils as they contain high concentrations of eucalyptol, a key compound for decongesting and relieving chills.
Buds: Eucalyptus buds are another prominent part used for essential oils due to their high eucalyptol content and fragrance, which helps in fighting off chills and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 30 grams of eucalyptus globulus leaves during early morning hours to maximize oil content.
Steam distill the leaves in a 10:1 water-to-herb ratio using a copper still at 100 degrees celsius.
Collect the resulting essential oil in a 10-milliliter container after a 3-hour distillation process.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2-micron filter to remove impurities and improve clarity.
Store the finished eucalyptus globulus essential oil in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with chills because of its natural anti-inflammatory and warming properties. The oil, extracted from ginger, contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol that stimulate circulation and raise body temperature. This helps to alleviate cold and flu symptoms, such as shivering and fever. Additionally, ginger's natural analgesic and antipyretic properties make it an effective remedy for soothing discomfort and reducing body heat. As a result, ginger essential oil can provide relief from chills and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Bisabolol: Helps with chills by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, reducing body temperature, and relieving symptoms of colds and flu.
Gingerols: Helps with chills by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving agent, reducing muscle and joint pain associated with colds and flu.
Zingiberene: Helps with chills by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and other conditions that cause chills.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhizomes are the most commonly used part to make essential oils for chills due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and warming properties.
Roots: Roots are also used to make essential oils for chills, as they contain similar compounds to rhyzomes and can be a more affordable alternative.
Leaves: Leaves are sometimes used to make essential oils for chills, although they may not be as effective as rhyzomes or roots due to lower concentrations of active compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes when the foliage starts to yellow and dry out, usually after 8-10 months.
Steam distill the rhizomes to extract the essential oil, using 100g of rhizomes per 500ml of distillation apparatus.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separating funnel within 2 hours.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove any remaining impurities or particles immediately.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils helps with chills because of its natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains compounds like carnosic acid and rosmanol, which help to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. By opening up airways and reducing mucus buildup, it facilitates easier breathing and can help to alleviate chills associated with colds, flu, or other illnesses. Additionally, its expectorant properties aid in the removal of excess mucus, providing relief from congestion and promoting a sense of comfort and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps with chills by acting as an antiviral agent, reducing the severity of cold and flu symptoms.
Bornyl acetate: Helps with chills by reducing inflammation and relieving respiratory congestion, which are often associated with chills.
Camphor: Helps with chills by acting as a vasodilator, increasing blood flow to the skin and helping to reduce fever and alleviate chills.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their high concentration of camphor, a compound that helps to relieve chills and fever.
Flowers: Flowers are used for their floral and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and calm the body during chills.
Stems: Stems are used due to their rich content of cineole, a compound that has decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties to help alleviate chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Steam distill the leaves in a 5-liter still with 3 liters of water at a temperature of 100°c for 3 hours.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the water and has a yield of 2-3 percent.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2-micron filter paper to remove any impurities and contaminants.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils helps with chills because it contains thymol, a natural decongestant that clears mucus from the body, promoting a healthy respiratory system. The oil's antimicrobial properties also help combat underlying infections that can cause chills. Additionally, its warming and stimulating effects can help to increase circulation and reduce body temperature, providing relief from the discomfort of chills. remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol helps with chills due to its antimicrobial properties, which can help combat underlying infections that may be causing the chills.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol helps with chills due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with chills.
Bornyl acetate: Bornyl acetate helps with chills due to its stimulatory effect on the body's thermoregulation, which can help increase body temperature and alleviate chills.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Thymus vulgaris contain thymol and carvacrol, which are antiseptic and antiviral properties that help to combat chills.
Flowers: Thymus vulgaris flowers contain thymol and carvacrol, which have decongestant and expectorant properties to relieve respiratory issues associated with chills.
Stems: The stems of Thymus vulgaris are rich in thymol and carvacrol, which have antibacterial and antifungal properties to help combat infections that cause chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers when the plant is in full bloom and the weather is warm and sunny.
Steam distill the fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers for 2-3 hours to release the essential oils.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separating funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter or a coffee filter to remove any impurities.
Bottle the filtered essential oil in dark glass containers and store it in a cool dry place away from light.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils helps with chills because it contains a compound called echinacoside, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_constituents: Cichoric acid: It helps with chills by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce the severity of cold and flu symptoms.
Caffeic acid: It helps with chills by possessing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can alleviate the discomfort associated with colds and flu.
Isobutyl amide: It helps with chills by acting as an analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, which can help reduce pain and discomfort caused by chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea are commonly used due to their high concentration of echinacoside, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate chills.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their ability to provide a calming effect, which can help reduce chills and promote overall well-being.
Flowers: The flowers are a popular choice for essential oils due to their high concentration of alkylamides, compounds that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried echinacea purpurea flowers in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Steam distill the harvested flowers with 500ml of water for 2 hours in a laboratory setting.
Collect and separate the essential oil from the distillate using a separator funnel and measure its volume.
Filter the collected essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove impurities and measure its final volume.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at a temperature below 25 degrees celsius.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oils helps with chills because of its natural warming properties and ability to stimulate circulation. The oil's active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, have been shown to increase blood flow and heat production, helping to counteract the cold and shivering sensations associated with chills. remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This constituent helps with chills by exhibiting analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce pain and discomfort associated with chills.
Cinnamyl acetate: This constituent helps with chills by displaying antipyretic properties, which help in reducing fever and relieving chills.
Eugenol: This constituent helps with chills by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which reduce swelling, pain, and the risk of infection associated with chills.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high concentration of eugenol, a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate chills.
Barks: The barks are used because they contain cinnamaldehyde, a compound known for its warming and anti-inflammatory properties, which help relieve chills.
Stems: The stems are used due to their high content of sesquiterpenes, which have been found to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature cinnamomum verum bark, carefully selecting pieces with high oil content, to ensure optimal results.
Dry the harvested cinnamomum verum bark in a warm, well-ventilated area for 2-3 weeks, or until crispy.
Grind the dried cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Steam distill the powder using a steam distillation apparatus for 3-4 hours, or until oil production slows.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or paper filter to remove any impurities.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils helps with chills because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that aid in reducing body temperature and soothing the body. The oil's antioxidant and antimicrobial properties work together to combat the underlying causes of chills, such as infections or autoimmune responses. remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate the symptoms of chills and fever.
Terpenoids: Terpenoids, such as bilobalide, help to increase blood flow and reduce inflammation, which can be beneficial in countering the effects of chills.
Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, including ginkgolides, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce the severity of chills and fever.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part for essential oil production due to their high concentration of ginkgolides, which provide anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties to help alleviate chills.
Seeds: The seeds are used to extract oil that contains ginkgotoxins, which have been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that can help combat chills and fever.

Roots: The roots are used for essential oil production and contain compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can aid in relieving chills and other symptoms associated with viral infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-100 fresh ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in early autumn for optimal oil content.
Steam distill the leaves for 2-3 hours at 100-110 degrees celsius to release essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation water using a separating funnel within 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove impurities within 10 minutes.
Store the ginkgo biloba essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight and heat.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils helps with chills because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as cineole and terpinen-4-ol, work together to combat underlying infections and reduce inflammation in the body. This helps to alleviate chills, fever, and other flu-like symptoms, promoting a sense of comfort and relaxation. By naturally soothing the body, Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils provide a natural and effective way to combat the discomfort associated with chills.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (also known as Eucalyptol): Acts as a natural decongestant, helping to relieve cold and flu symptoms, including chills.
Limonene: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing fever and alleviating chills associated with infections.
Caryophyllene: Displays antimicrobial properties, helping to combat underlying infections that may be causing chills.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in cineole, a compound known for its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective against chills.
Buds: Contain high levels of cineole, which helps to reduce fever and alleviate chills.
Leaves: Also contain other compounds like terpinen-4-ol, which helps to relieve respiratory issues associated with chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves and stems from a healthy plant, preferably in the early morning.
Steam distill the harvested plant material in a stainless steel still with a 1:3 plant-to-water ratio for 2 hours.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water using a condenser and a separator, then filter the oil through a coffee filter.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, away from heat and light, at a temperature below 25°c.
Inspect the essential oil for its color, consistency, and aroma, and discard it if it shows signs of oxidation or spoilage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils helps with chills because it is rich in compounds such as limonene and geraniol, which have natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties work to open up airways and reduce inflammation in the body, making it easier to breathe and warm up. Additionally, the invigorating and refreshing scent of the oil can help to increase circulation and reduce the feeling of chilliness, providing a sense of warmth and comfort. This makes Cymbopogon citratus essential oil a popular remedy for colds and flu.
remedy_constituents: Citral: This compound helps to reduce chills by promoting sweating and warmness due to its thermogenic properties.
Limonene: It aids in reducing chills by acting as a natural decongestant and promoting a sense of warmth and comfort.
Geranial: This compound helps to alleviate chills by its antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in reducing fever and associated symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of limonene, a compound known for its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: They are used for their citral content, which helps to reduce fever and relieve chills.
Rhyzomes: They are used for their high concentration of geranial and neral, compounds that possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, helping to combat chills and fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh stalks of cymbopogon citratus at dawn when the dew is still present on the leaves.
Steam distill the harvested stalks in a copper still with a 20:1 water to plant ratio for 3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a glass container and filter it through a cotton cloth to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its aroma.
Allow the essential oil to mature for 24 hours before using it for perfumery or aromatherapy purposes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with chills because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's primary compound, linalool, has been shown to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making it an effective remedy for soothing chills. Its calming scent also helps to calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate chills. Furthermore, the oil's antispasmodic properties help to ease muscle tension, allowing the body to recover from illness and reducing the discomfort associated with chills.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with chills.
Linalyl acetate: As a terpene, linalyl acetate contributes to the oil's antipyretic and analgesic effects, helping to lower body temperature and ease discomfort caused by chills.
Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate the discomfort associated with chills.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used to make essential oils for chills due to their high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
Leaves: Used to make essential oils for chills due to their ability to reduce stress and promote relaxation.
Stems: Used to make essential oils for chills due to their high content of linalool, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning when they are dry and free of dew.
Steam distill the harvested flowers using a ratio of 1 part plant material to 3 parts distilled water for 4 hours.
Collect the distilled water vapor as it rises and cools resulting in a mixture of essential oil and water.
Separate the essential oil from the water by decanting it into a separate container over a period of 30 minutes.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight fitting lid to preserve its aroma for up to 1 year.
